message:
fix(h2): refuse new streams after GOAWAY per RFC 9113 §6.8

After sending GOAWAY with last_stream_id=N, the server's process_headers
still accepted incoming HEADERS with stream_id > N, creating new streams
during the drain phase. A misbehaving peer could exploit this to open
unbounded streams after GOAWAY.

Fix: check goaway_sent && frame.stream_id > last_stream_id at the top
of process_headers, and send RST_STREAM with REFUSED_STREAM (0x7)
instead of creating the stream.

Found via end-to-end flow tracing of the GOAWAY→drain lifecycle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
